definitions.text: To intend. To intend, to plan (to do); to have as one's intention. .mw-parser-output .defdate{font-size:smaller}
examples: I didn't mean to knock your tooth out.
I mean to go to Arévalo in Spain this summer; I’ve been meaning to tell you for weeks, but I’ve just found the time.

definitions.text: To intend, to plan (to do); to have as one's intention. .mw-parser-output .defdate{font-size:smaller}
examples: I didn't mean to knock your tooth out.
I mean to go to Arévalo in Spain this summer; I’ve been meaning to tell you for weeks, but I’ve just found the time.

definitions.text: To have as intentions of a given kind.
examples: Don't be angry; she meant well.

definitions.text: Of a common or low origin, grade, or quality; common; humble.
Low in quality or degree; inferior; poor; shabby.
Having the mean as its value; average.
A method or course of action used to achieve some result.
An intermediate step or intermediate steps.
Something which is intermediate or in the middle; an intermediate value or range of values; a medium.
